map_items.title: Self-directed search before mentor input
map_items.summary: P-01 prefers to explore independently before accepting mentor direction.
map_items.excerpt: I want a mentor nearby, but only after I have wrestled with the problem enough to know what I am asking.
map_items.anonymized_text: I want a mentor nearby, but only after I have wrestled with the problem enough to know what I am asking.

metadata_json.inference_note: Shows desire for autonomy before guidance.

map_items.title: Mentor sees selective receptivity
map_items.summary: Mentor note suggests P-01 acts on advice once it is framed as an experiment.
map_items.excerpt: They resist direct prescriptions, but respond well when advice is framed as a testable constraint.
map_items.anonymized_text: They resist direct prescriptions, but respond well when advice is framed as a testable constraint.

map_items.title: Public certainty about founder identity
map_items.summary: Public post presents decisiveness that contrasts with private uncertainty.
map_items.excerpt: Building in public has forced clarity: we know exactly which user pain we are solving this quarter.
map_items.anonymized_text: Building in public has forced clarity: we know exactly which user pain we are solving this quarter.

map_items.title: Private uncertainty under confident updates
map_items.summary: Reflection notes a gap between public certainty and internal ambiguity.
map_items.excerpt: The updates sound clean, but privately I still feel like the company changes shape every week.
map_items.anonymized_text: The updates sound clean, but privately I still feel like the company changes shape every week.

map_items.title: Needs structure before execution
map_items.summary: P-02 describes needing clear milestones before moving quickly.
map_items.excerpt: Once the week has a concrete target, I can execute hard. Without that, I spend too much time arranging the work.
map_items.anonymized_text: Once the week has a concrete target, I can execute hard. Without that, I spend too much time arranging the work.

map_items.title: Sprint template improves follow-through
map_items.summary: Program material gives P-02 a repeatable weekly structure.
map_items.excerpt: Weekly sprint board: one customer-learning goal, one build milestone, one decision log entry.
map_items.anonymized_text: Weekly sprint board: one customer-learning goal, one build milestone, one decision log entry.

map_items.title: Earlier pattern of over-preparing
map_items.summary: Prior interview shows P-02 delayed outreach until the plan felt complete.
map_items.excerpt: I kept tuning the outreach list because sending the first ten messages felt like committing to the wrong version.
map_items.anonymized_text: I kept tuning the outreach list because sending the first ten messages felt like committing to the wrong version.

map_items.title: High ambiguity tolerance with mentor skepticism
map_items.summary: P-03 is comfortable with uncertainty but cautious about external advice.
map_items.excerpt: Ambiguity does not bother me. What bothers me is advice that arrives before someone understands the weird parts.
map_items.anonymized_text: Ambiguity does not bother me. What bothers me is advice that arrives before someone understands the weird parts.

map_items.title: Reflective independence
map_items.summary: P-03 frames independence as a way to protect the early idea from premature narrowing.
map_items.excerpt: I am not rejecting help; I am protecting the question long enough to see what it wants to become.
map_items.anonymized_text: I am not rejecting help; I am protecting the question long enough to see what it wants to become.

map_items.title: Public story hides exploratory reality
map_items.summary: P-03's public positioning is sharper than the private exploratory process.
map_items.excerpt: We are focused on one wedge: make expert workflows feel lightweight enough to repeat every day.
map_items.anonymized_text: We are focused on one wedge: make expert workflows feel lightweight enough to repeat every day.

map_items.title: Mentor trust tied to operating cadence
map_items.summary: P-04 trusts mentors who create repeatable operating rhythm.
map_items.excerpt: The best mentor does not give me inspiration. They make the next operating loop obvious.
map_items.anonymized_text: The best mentor does not give me inspiration. They make the next operating loop obvious.

map_items.title: Converts feedback into tasks
map_items.summary: Mentor note shows P-04 rapidly operationalizes advice.
map_items.excerpt: They translated feedback into a three-step sales test within the same session.
map_items.anonymized_text: They translated feedback into a three-step sales test within the same session.

questions.query: Who is self-directed but ambivalent about mentorship?
questions.question_text: Who is self-directed but ambivalent about mentorship?

answer.synthesis: Inference: P-01 and P-03 both show strong self-direction with selective or cautious use of mentorship. P-01 wants to form the question independently before receiving input; P-03 protects ambiguity until advice feels context-aware.
answer.follow_up: Which mentor formats preserve autonomy while still increasing execution speed?

questions.query: Where do public and private founder identities diverge?
questions.question_text: Where do public and private founder identities diverge?

answer.synthesis: Inference: P-01 and P-03 present crisp public narratives while private reflections preserve uncertainty. The divergence is strongest around external clarity versus internal exploration.
answer.follow_up: Does public narrative pressure help founders focus, or does it prematurely narrow exploration?

questions.query: Which participants need structure before they can execute?
questions.question_text: Which participants need structure before they can execute?

answer.synthesis: Inference: P-02 most clearly needs structure before execution; P-04 also values structured cadence, but uses it as an accelerator rather than a prerequisite.
answer.follow_up: Which program rituals reduce decision latency without creating dependency?

questions.query: What kinds of mentorship are trusted?
questions.question_text: What kinds of mentorship are trusted?

answer.synthesis: Inference: Trust increases when mentors convert advice into experiments, constraints, or operating loops. Direct prescriptions are less trusted by autonomy-oriented participants.
answer.follow_up: Can mentor recommendations be represented as map actions tied to evidence clusters?
